title: Allen: 'Bat at No.1' instruction, MLC carnage and making of Kiwi destroyer
content: Finn Allen transformed from a middle-order batter into New Zealand’s explosive T20 opener after Wellington coach Glenn Pocknall promoted him to open in 2020. Recognising his power and attacking potential, Pocknall nurtured Allen’s aggressive style through challenging training. Allen later proved the decision right with record-breaking performances, including a 33-ball T20 World Cup century and a 151-run MLC blitz.

title: Delhi tourist spends night hiding in Nainital forest after rape bid by taxi driver
content: A woman's solo trip to Nainital turned terrifying when her taxi driver attempted to sexually assault her. She escaped into a forest, hiding overnight until rescued by villagers. The accused was arrested and confessed to the crime.

title: T20 World Cup 2026: Ahmedabad, the final, and the ghost of November 19 past
content: Silence and crowd noise were back in discussion ahead of Sunday's final but India captain Suryakumar Yadav chose to downplay the chatter. He acknowledged the challenges, pressure and excitement a final brings, and his message to the team was simple: be courageous.

title: Happy International Women's Day: 75+ messages, greetings, wishes and quotes
content: International Women's Day prompts reflection on the vital roles women play, often quietly.  The article highlights the significance of acknowledging their strength, effort, and presence.  It offers a collection of messages, wishes, and quotes to help express appreciation for the women who enrich our lives, encouraging a pause to recognize their impact.

title: Man held for strangling woman over marriage, money dispute; body dumped on Noida service road
content: A Delhi driver was arrested for allegedly strangling a woman to death with her scarf following a financial dispute. The victim, Kavita, met the driver, Vicky Mathur, and offered him money to marry her, but he refused. Mathur then allegedly strangled her, stole her cash, and dumped her body in Noida.

title: Boy goes on rampage at Karnataka school, attacks sleeping students with bed rod; 1 dead
content: A shocking incident unfolded at a Gurukul residential school in Ballari, Karnataka, where a boy allegedly attacked fellow students with a bed rod late at night. The assault resulted in one student's death and left eight others injured, with two sustaining serious wounds. All injured students were promptly taken to a hospital for medical attention.

title: Unions representing 700,000 workers ask Amazon, Google, Microsoft to reject AI for war
content: Employees from major tech firms like Amazon, Google, and Microsoft are demanding their companies reject Pentagon contracts that could compromise AI safety guardrails.  The open letter, from 'No Tech For Apartheid,' urges leaders to refuse demands that would allow AI for mass surveillance and autonomous weaponry, citing concerns over complicity in violence and repression.

title: Is US responsible for Iran school bombing that killed 160? What Trump said
content: President Trump declared the US has eliminated Iran's entire leadership, describing it as removing a "cancer." He asserted US forces have decimated Iran's navy, air force, and missile capabilities, claiming victory in the ongoing conflict. Trump denied US responsibility for a school bombing, blaming Iran's inaccuracy.

title: Perennial dark horses, really? Why world keeps forgetting NZ’s World Cup pedigree
content: Nine ODI World Cup semi-finals. Two finals. Five T20 World Cup semi-finals and now a second final on Sunday. New Zealand’s record in big-ticket tournaments is anything but accidental, yet they continue to be casually described as cricket’s perennial dark horses.

title: Watch: Israel strikes several oil depots in Iran; towering smoke, massive blaze light up skyline
content: Israeli forces struck fuel storage complexes in Tehran and Karaj, causing large explosions and fires. The military stated these sites were used for Iran's armed forces, calling it a significant strike to dismantle military infrastructure. Iran's Revolutionary Guards Corps announced retaliatory attacks on Israel.

title: Amazon sends letter to FCC saying: Reject application of Elon Musk's Spacex for Space data centers
content: Amazon's Project Kuiper has urged the FCC to reject SpaceX's proposal for a million-satellite constellation, citing a lack of essential technical and orbital parameters. Amazon argues the plan is speculative, lacks space safety details, and would take centuries to deploy, potentially hoarding orbital resources.

title: '80,000 watching live': Watch moment YouTuber crashes Fortuner on Delhi–Meerut Expressway
content: Motovlogger Anurag Dobhal, known as UK07 Rider, livestreamed his SUV crashing at high speed on the Delhi-Meerut Expressway. Visibly distressed and speaking of loneliness, he steered his vehicle into the highway divider.  Dobhal, who had spoken of personal and family disputes, sustained injuries and fractures but is stable.

title: From oil to rice, here's how Middle East crisis may spread across global economy
content: The Middle East conflict is escalating, triggering global economic concerns.  Disruptions to trade, energy supplies, and food production are already evident, with potential for significant inflation and reduced growth.  Asian economies and global shipping networks face particular vulnerability as tensions persist.

title: Watch: Bengaluru apartment residents come to blows over dog walk
content: A morning dog walk in Bengaluru's Varthur escalated into a physical clash between residents at Brigade Cornerstone Utopia. A dispute over a pet owner using a designated exercise area led to accusations of abuse, phone snatching, and assault. Police have registered cross-complaints and are investigating the incident.

title: 'Not helping them much': Trump downplays reports of Russia sharing intel with Iran
content: Trump downplayed reports of Russia sharing intelligence with Iran to target US forces, stating it "is not helping them much" given recent events. He suggested that even if such sharing occurred, Iran has gained little, and dismissed concerns about US-Russia relations. Trump also indicated the US would not immediately tap its Strategic Petroleum Reserve to address rising oil prices.

title: Khamenei gone but regime change in Iran 'unlikely', US intelligence warns — report
content: A joint US-Israeli attack killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, fulfilling President Trump's "regime change" calls. However, a classified intelligence report suggests this is unlikely to topple the entrenched clerical and military establishment, even with a large-scale assault. Analysts concluded Iran's system would likely continue functioning due to established continuity procedures.

title: 'Spoiled narcissist': Taylor Swift faces online criticism about Joe Alwyn
content: A viral social media post criticizing Taylor Swift has sparked debate online, referencing her past relationship with Joe Alwyn and mentioning Travis Kelce. The post accuses Swift of belittling Alwyn’s struggles and mocks her current relationship. While the comments spread quickly online, they reflect one user’s opinion rather than verified claims about the singer.

title: LeBron James’ wife Savannah shares teen parenting advice on podcast
content: Savannah James, wife of NBA star LeBron James, shared her golden rule for teenage parenting on her podcast 'Everybody's Crazy.' She advocates for raising children to be capable adults and then allowing them to explore life independently, while still offering support as a safety net.​ Savannah James makes sure everything is going great with their kids at home. Both Bronny and Bryce Maximus are currently walking on their legendary dad’s path, whereas daughter Zhuri Nova is knee-deep into volleyball.

title: Andrew Mangiapane reacts as Oilers trade sends him to Blackhawks before deadline
content: Andrew Mangiapane has also discussed his departure from the Edmonton Oilers when the winger was traded to the Chicago Blackhawks early on before the NHL trade deadline. The transfer is after a tough season for the forward in Edmonton and provides him with a new chance in Chicago, where the two clubs rebuild their line-up.

title: NFL Trade News: Patriots, Colts linked to $50M Bengals DE
content: Sports Illustrated’s Gilberto Manzano projects Cincinnati Bengals defensive end Trey Hendrickson for a three-year, $105M deal with the New England Patriots and Indianapolis Colts as potential landing spots. New England needs pass rush help for their quarterback Drake Maye. And Indianapolis seeks to improve their defense under former Cincinnati defensive coordinator Lou Anarumo.  The Colts may free up cap space by trading or releasing their wide receiver Michael Pittman Jr., who is in the final year of his three-year contract.

title: Bengaluru woman ends life after argument with husband over sambar
content: A 25-year-old homemaker in Bengaluru died by suicide after consuming a grain-preserving tablet following a dispute with her husband over serving leftover sambar. The argument escalated when the husband scolded her for not preparing fresh food on the third day of serving the same dish. Her father has requested a police investigation into the circumstances.

title: 'Just in the dark': De Kock slams ICC as England leaves but SA, WI remain stuck
content: Quinton de Kock slammed the ICC over perceived preferential treatment for England's departure from the T20 World Cup.  While England is set to fly home soon after their semi-final exit, South Africa and West Indies, eliminated earlier, remain stranded in India awaiting charter flights.  This has sparked accusations of unequal influence among cricket nations.

title: 2 college friends end lives inside Gujarat temple washroom
content: Two college friends, aged 18 and 20, were found dead in a temple washroom in Surat after allegedly injecting themselves with anesthetic drugs. Police discovered drug vials and syringes, along with mobile phone data indicating searches for suicide methods. The girls had left for college and did not return home.

title: How a Hyderabad man built a massive food distribution network while working 9-5
content: A Hyderabad  manager, Muhammad Aziz, dedicates his mornings for a decade to feeding the needy outside NIMS Hospital. What began with 30 packets has grown into a daily service for hundreds, providing nutritious, home-cooked meals. Aziz's unwavering commitment, supported by volunteers, ensures no one goes hungry, transforming a personal promise into a powerful movement of compassion.

title: Watch: Video shows passengers raising hands as man is dragged off flight after security scare
content: A Southwest Airlines flight en route to Fort Lauderdale made an emergency landing in Atlanta Friday night due to a security threat. Passengers were filmed with hands raised as police boarded the aircraft and removed a man. The incident caused a dramatic disruption to the flight's original route.

title: Delhi temperature crosses 35°C in March’s first week for first time in 15 years
content: Delhi experienced an unusually early heatwave as temperatures soared to 35.7°C, seven degrees above normal, marking the earliest March heat of this magnitude in 15 years. Clear skies and dry weather are fueling the rise, with forecasts predicting a further climb to 37°C. Nights are also warmer than usual, contributing to the discomfort.

title: Murder in Delhi’s Uttam Nagar: Kin demands CBI probe, claims 20 people involved; cops arrest 6
content: A 26-year-old man was allegedly lynched in Delhi's Uttam Nagar following a Holi celebration dispute. His father is demanding a CBI probe, citing a lack of transparency from the police. Six individuals, identified as wall painters and relatives, have been arrested in connection with the incident.

title: ‘To take a little pressure off’: Trump on waiver for India to buy Russian oil
content: The US may ease pressure on global oil markets, potentially allowing India to continue buying Russian crude to stabilize supply amid Middle East tensions. Washington granted India a temporary waiver to accept Russian oil shipments, ensuring energy security as crucial shipping routes face disruptions. India assures consumers of comfortable energy supplies, with diversified imports and adequate reserves.

title: 'Gambhir fought everyone': Shoaib Akhtar's stunning reveal on Samson's comeback
content: Former Pakistan bowler Shoaib Akhtar commends India coach Gautam Gambhir.  Gambhir strongly advocated for Sanju Samson's inclusion in the T20 World Cup squad. Samson has since delivered exceptional performances.  His batting has been a major highlight of the tournament.  He has scored significantly and broken records.  This has helped India reach the final.

title: Gulf crisis: Dubai's Marina Tower hit by debris from intercepted Iranian projectile
content: Dubai authorities confirmed that debris from a successful aerial interception, not a direct attack, caused a minor incident in Dubai Marina. The event was linked to regional tensions and ongoing conflict involving Iran, the United States and Israel and resulted in falling debris that struck a tower, with no injuries reported.

title: Netanyahu vows to continue Iran war ‘with all our force’
content: Israel vows to eradicate Iran's regime, escalating strikes on Tehran and military targets. Iran retaliates across the Gulf, hitting US bases and warning allies. The conflict, now in its second week after the deaths of Iran's Supreme Leader and US strikes, has spread regionally, causing global market jitters and rising casualties.

title: Gwalior: 20-year-old woman abducted from street, gang-raped on college campus
content: A 20-year-old woman was abducted and gang-raped by three men on a college campus in Gwalior's Dabra area. The incident, which occurred on February 19, came to light after the survivor's health worsened. Police have arrested the three accused, identified as a watchman and two e-rickshaw drivers.

title: China asserts need for more power as Donald Trump embraces war
content: Chinese analysts believe the US would not target Xi Jinping like it did Khamenei due to China's nuclear status. However, Trump's embrace of war reinforces Beijing's view of the US as its primary threat, prompting calls for superior firepower and self-reliance. China also warns against internal threats and deceptive negotiations with Washington.

title: Uttar Pradesh: 2.4 crore names dropped in poll rolls since SIR announced
content: Uttar Pradesh's voter count is approaching 13 crore. A voter roll revision exercise is nearing completion. Notices were issued and hearings held for millions of voters. Many names were removed due to migration or other reasons. Thousands of applications for corrections and updates were also processed. The final electoral roll will be published soon.

title: India doesn't need permission to buy Russian oil, say officials
content: India continues to source crude oil from Russia, asserting its right to procure from any supplier based on national interest, affordability, and availability. Government sources clarified that recent US waivers were for domestic audiences, as New Delhi's energy security is not solely dependent on single maritime routes like the Strait of Hormuz, with a significant portion of imports unaffected.

title: New Zealand aim to 'silence' Ahmedabad crowd but wary of Jasprit Bumrah
content: New Zealand captain Mitchell Santner is determined to "break a few hearts" by defeating defending champions India in the T20 World Cup final.

title: Inside Pawan Kalyan’s luxurious Hyderabad residence: Theatre, gym, pool and more
content: Pawan Kalyan's opulent Jubilee Hills residence in Hyderabad boasts luxurious amenities like a private theatre and pool, blending traditional and modern design. The actor, with an estimated net worth of Rs 450 crore from acting, endorsements, and politics, has also completed his upcoming film 'Ustaad Bhagat Singh', reportedly taking a reduced fee.

title: Peaceful Nepal elections historic, ready to work with new government: PM Modi
content: Nepal's elections saw a landslide victory for Balendra Shah's Rastriya Swatantra Party. Prime Minister Narendra Modi congratulated Nepal on its peaceful polls, highlighting the vibrant exercise of democratic rights. Shah, a rapper-turned-politician, projects a 'Nepal First' hyper-nationalism, emphasizing neutrality in foreign policy.

title: PMO seeks third-party audits for road, rail projects
content: The PMO is pushing for independent third-party audits in road and rail projects to boost quality and speed up execution. Ministries are tasked with studying past successful construction practices, like those from the Golden Quadrilateral era, and international models from Indonesia and Malaysia.

title: Dera Sacha Sauda chief Gurmeet Ram Rahim acquitted in journalist murder case
content: Punjab and Haryana High Court acquitted Dera Sacha Sauda chief Gurmeet Ram Rahim in the 2002 murder of journalist Ram Chander Chhatrapati, overturning a life sentence. Ram Rahim remains jailed for rape convictions, and his family plans to challenge the acquittal in the Supreme Court.

title: Gulf conflict: Indians in Bahrain, Qatar eye Saudi visas to fly out
content: Indians in Qatar and Bahrain are seeking Saudi transit visas as a backup plan due to flight cancellations. With airports in Doha and Bahrain largely shut, many are preparing for overland journeys to Saudi Arabia or Oman. This comes amid ongoing regional tensions, prompting a need for alternative travel routes home.

title: LB Mack returning to Chargers on one-year deal
content: Nine-time Pro Bowler Khalil Mack reached a one-year deal to remain with the Chargers, with sources telling ESPN it's worth $18 million.

title: Skubal fans five, allows leadoff HR as U.S. wins
content: Great Britain's Nate Eaton greeted Team USA pitcher Tarik Skubal with a home run on the first pitch of the game Saturday, as the defending American League Cy Young Award winner made his only start of the World Baseball Classic in Houston, a 9-1 U.S. win.

title: Hurley tossed as UConn stumbles in 1-seed push
content: UConn coach Danny Hurley was ejected in the final second as Marquette hung on for a 68-62 victory that prevented the fourth-ranked Huskies from winning a share of the Big East regular-season title and hurt their hopes for a 1-seed in the NCAA tournament.

title: Messi paid $70M-$80M per year, Miami owner says
content: Inter Miami co-owner Jorge Mas revealed that captain Lionel Messi is paid between $70 million and $80 million a year, including the player's ownership shares.

title: Topuria-Gaethje, Pereira-Gane top UFC's WH card
content: A lightweight title bout between Ilia Topuria and Justin Gaethje will headline the UFC's White House event on June 14, while Alex Pereira and Ciryl Gane will vie for the interim heavyweight championship, the promotion announced Saturday.

title: Crosby bids adieu to Vegas, excited about Ravens
content: Longtime Raiders star Maxx Crosby, who was traded to the Ravens on Friday, expressed his appreciation for his time with Mark Davis' franchise in a video published to his social media accounts Saturday.

title: UTEP fan's clash with JSU players under review
content: The University of Texas-El Paso, Jacksonville State and Conference USA will review an altercation that appeared to involve a UTEP fan picking up a chair in a fiery exchange with Jacksonville State players on Saturday.

title: Albies' walk-off leads Netherlands to WBC stunner
content: Braves star Ozzie Albies slugged a walk-off three-run home run Saturday to lead the Netherlands to a dramatic win over Nicaragua at the World Baseball Classic.

title: Women's Bracketology: With Vandy out of running, who can grab final No. 1 seed?
content: Vanderbilt's pursuit of a 1-seed is over. But as many as four teams still have a shot at the final No. 1 seed.

title: Solak: Why Crosby fits perfectly with the Ravens' D -- and their Super Bowl window
content: Baltimore had to give up two first-round picks to get him, but Crosby can dominate in the Ravens' defense.

title: Australian Grand Prix LIVE: Latest updates as Leclerc leads Hamilton, Russell
content: George Russell starts on pole for the Australian Grand Prix on Sunday as drivers battle for the opening race win of the season, and you can follow it all live on ESPN.

title: How Duke's Krzyzewskiville ordering test decides who gets the best 'Cameron Crazies' seats
content: There aren't many tests at Duke that will produce a median score among takers of 43.2%. But Krzyzewskiville's annual "ordering test" isn't just any test.

title: Bitterness turns into celebration as Puerto Rico's WBC journey begins
content: Insurance controversy rocked the roster, but the home team in Pool A of the World Baseball Classic is writing its next chapter.

title: Predictions: Will Japan repeat or is United States the team to beat?
content: Our experts make their picks for every round of the 2026 WBC -- from pool play through the championship game.

title: UFC 326: Live results, analysis for Holloway vs. Oliveira 2
content: Brett Okamoto, Andreas Hale and Jeff Wagenheim relay results and live analysis from UFC 326 in Las Vegas.

title: Wrexham dreaming of the Premier League? Chelsea give them a reality check
content: Wrexham are looking to get promoted to the Premier League, but their FA Cup loss to Chelsea gives them a reminder of what they will expect should they get promoted.

title: The NFL's most overrated, underrated free agents: Why Travis Etienne Jr. might be a risky signing
content: As NFL free agency begins next week, we picked out five free agents who might be flying under the radar -- and five who are getting too much hype.

title: Reid's updated NFL draft QB Hot Board: Strengths and weaknesses for the top 12 passers
content: Who are the best quarterbacks in the 2026 class? We sized up the top 12, starting with Fernando Mendoza and Ty Simpson.

title: Who aced the 2026 NHL trade deadline? Six winners, six losers
content: The final week was a fun one, with some late shockers. Here's who feels great now -- and who might not.

title: Stanley Cup playoff watch: Resetting the postseason races after the deadline
content: Playoff spots, division titles and seeds are up for grabs. Here are projections, draft lottery standings and more.

title: F1 drivers eviscerate new cars: What's going wrong, and is there a quick fix?
content: F1's new cars failed to deliver on a painful debut as Lando Norris and Co. raged at the driving experience - ESPN's Nate Saunders makes sense of the situation.

title: Russell takes pole as Mercedes looks dominant
content: George Russell delivered on his status as the world championship favorite by taking a crushing pole position.

title: The Alex Eala Effect: How a 20-year-old phenom has taken the tennis world by storm
content: Filipina tennis prodigy Alex Eala has drawn enormous crowds everywhere she goes. On Sunday, she will take on Coco Gauff at Indian Wells.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Next Tottenham manager candidates emerge
content: Tottenham Hotspur could replace manager Igor Tudor soon, while Barcelona eye Spurs' most promising young defender. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Steve Emt's incredible story: From UConn basketball to three-time Paralympian
content: Former UConn basketball player Steve Emt was paralyzed in a car accident at 25. Then he discovered his immense skill in Paralympic curling.

title: International Women’s Day: Educated, empowered women pillars of progressive nation, says Droupadi Murmu
content: As 'Nari Shakti' continues to excel across diverse fields and lead with courage, it strengthens the foundations of a more inclusive and prosperous society, she said

title: In Chennai, a former Commissioner’s name lives on, in different forms
content: Nearly ten years since historian V. Sriram pointed out that two road name boards would cause Sir Eric Conran-Smith to turn in his grave, those twin errors persist

title: Mercury rises across north; Delhi records highest maximum temperature for first week of March in 50 years
content: According to weather data from the past 50 years, earlier the highest maximum temperature reading during the first seven days of March at Safdarjung, the city's base weather station, was 34.8°C logged on March 5, 1999.

title: 45% Kashmir students up to Class 12 report sub-optimal mental well-being due to social media: study
content: “The inability of some students to check in with some technologies according to their wishes results in a feeling of anxiety. More than 14% were moderately to highly anxious when they were unable to use text messages,” according to the study, published this year by the Ianna Journal of Interdisciplinary Studies

title: Delhi court grants two-week interim bail to Al-Falah chairman Jawed Siddiqui in money laundering case
content: “After considering all the present facts and circumstances, I am of the considered view that the applicant/ accused deserves to be enlarged on interim bail as the wife of applicant/ accused is unwell,” the court said in its order

title: Why India’s ‘leaky pipeline’ in research is unlike the rest of the world
content: Most women PhD holders in STEM in India find themselves unable to access long-term, lucrative, and prestigious research jobs

title: Delhi woman hides in Uttarakhand forest overnight to escape rape attempt by taxi driver
content: To save herself, the woman ran towards the woods and hid in the forest overnight. She somehow managed to reach Patwa Dangar village on Friday (March 6) morning, and with the help of locals, filed a police report.

title: Many colorectal cancer patients in India diagnosed at advanced stage, finds Kidwai study
content: Doctors urge people not to ignore warning signs such as persistent abdominal pain, changes in bowel habits, blood in the stool, unexplained weight loss or prolonged fatigue

title: Onwards and upwards: women of Tamil Nadu claim their space in local administration
content: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1992 mandates at least 33% (one-third) reservation for women in Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) across India, including for positions of chairpersons. The HinduC. Palanivel Rajan takes a deep dive to examine whether women have moved from being seen as “proxies” or “rubber-stamp” heads to achieving true empowerment. Hearteningly, hewe find that many women leaders have indeed wrested agency and truereal power.

title: West Asia conflict: Over 52,000 Indians returned between March 1-7 after partial opening of airspace
content: The MEA said Indian missions in the region have issued detailed advisories and set up 24x7 helplines to assist Indians; more flights planned in coming days

title: ‘Cannot be dismissed as logistical issues’: Odisha CM expresses anguish over shifting of tribal meet venue in Bengal
content: CM Mohan Majhi’s reaction came close on the heels of rare public displeasure expressed by President Droupadi Murmu over shifting of venue for the International Santhal Conclave in Darjeeling

title: Indian students stuck in Iran fear for safety; families plead for evacuation
content: Places in Iran to which they are being relocated are no longer safe, bringing them back to India is the only solution, says AIMSA; evacuation via Armenia being considered from where students could ‘arrange and fund their return’ to India

title: TNExpat platform launched to provide expats a peek into life, culture in T.N.
content: Tamil Nadu Industries Minister T.R.B. Rajaa inaugurates the platform; the website is currently available in five languages, making it accessible to a wider international community

title: Tamil Nadu releases Government Order to regulate cosmetology, aesthetics, and hair transplant clinics
content: The order is based on the National Medical Commission guidelines on aesthetics and hair transplant issued on September 20, 2022, which have been incorporated into Tamil Nadu Clinical Establishment rules

title: There is no repugnancy between provisions of RERA and Karnataka Apartment Ownership Act: Karnataka High Court
content: ‘Association of owners formed under KAOA is the right body to manage residential apartments, not cooperative societies’

title: Rewriting Kerala-Africa migration narratives: towards a women’s gaze
content: Africa has lived in Kerala’s imagination through stories of trade, migration, adventure, and opportunity. However, most of these accounts were written through a male gaze. Now, women travellers are rewriting these narratives

title: Young women duo rev up a four-decade-old family garage in Kottayam
content: Amritha and Shruthimol have been working at ‘Ringoos Auto Works’ at Ponknnum town in  Kottayam, owned by Sivadasan, their father-in-law, for over three years now

title: Women entrepreneurs face disparity in fundraising
content: ‘Women founders continue to build resilient, high-potential businesses, yet the share of capital reaching them remains disproportionately small’

title: Rahul Gandhi offers five guarantees to people of Kerala ahead of Assembly polls
content: The five ‘welfare guarantees’  were aimed at giving a social security cover for the people of Kerala, especially the women and the youth, says Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha. Increase in welfare pensions for the marginalised, free travel for women on KSRTC buses are some of the guarantees

title: Iran-Israel conflict: CBSE postpones Class 12 board exam in West Asia
content: The board has cancelled Class 10 exams till March 11 and said the mode of declaration of results will be announced separately

title: Vijay promises ₹2,500 monthly assistance for women, free bus travel
content: Speaking at the International Women’s Day celebrations organised by the party in Mamallapuram, he hits out at the ruling party and claims that 30% of the budgetary allocation in T.N. is being siphoned off for the Chief Minister and his Cabinet

title: Maharashtra Government inks MoU with Finnish firms to develop Sassoon Dock
content: Sassoon Dock records an average annual fish production of 50,000 to 60,000 metric tonnes, making it a priority for the Maharashtra government, State Fisheries Minister Nitesh Rane said

title: Since 2017, cases of SCs denied access to public spaces on the rise with lion’s share in U.P.
content: From the latest available set of crime data in the NCRB’s 2023 Crime in India report, there were 180 reported cases of SCs being denied access to public spaces under the Prevention of Atrocities (SC/ST) Act across the country. Of these, 173 cases were reported in U.P. alone

title: Manipur BJP MLA’s burial yet to take place, 15 days after death
content: Vungzagin Valte was left paralysed by a mob attack in Imphal in May 2023; Before conducting last rites, Valte family, supporters demand NIA probe into the assault, and a political solution to the conflict

title: Six wagons of coal-laden goods train derail in MP’s Katni, distrupting rail traffic
content: Railway officials and staff rushed to the spot after receiving information and began assessing the situation

title: Kerala govt. brings out draft notification revising minimum wages of employees in State’s private health sector
content: The proposed revised wages sits lower than the basic pay hike being demanded by a large section nurses in the State

title: Intruders will be driven out from Kedarnath to Kanyakumari, says Amit Shah
content: Says names of those who are not Indian citizens must be removed from the voter list, as a clean electoral roll is essential for safeguarding democracy

title: Govt. mulling deploying Indian Navy to escort ships stranded in Persian Gulf: Govt. source
content: Decision likely in two days; govt source says Iranian promise of not attacking neighbours may facilitate ship movement across Strait of Hormuz

title: LPG hike: Home cooking and eating out expected to cost more
content: Bangalore Hotels Association says the Union government has recently also stopped the discount on MRP for bulk buyers of commercial LPG cylinders

title: Kerala Chief Minister dedicates State’s longest backwater bridge
content: The Perumbalam bridge in Alappuzha ends the long wait for a direct road link for Perumbalam islanders to the mainland

title: Operation Cy-Hunt 2.0: 46 cases over cyber financial fraud booked, 26 arrested in district in a day
content: While the Ernakulam Rural Police arrested 16 alleged offenders, the Kochi City Police nabbed 10 accused during the day-long drive

title: Plea for CM’s intervention to ensure rapid-acting insulin for children under Mittayi
content: Type One Diabetes Foundation (Kerala) alleges that the insulin currently provided is causing health problems among children

title: Normalcy returns to Kashmir after six days; DGP says sustained pressure put on anti-national elements
content: Concertina wires placed on roads and interior lanes in parts of Srinagar city, Budgam and Baramulla were also removed, allowing vehicular traffic to ply smoothly on the roads; locals said internet speed, which was reduced to 2G service, and prepaid mobile calling were also restored

title: No foreign capital should dictate New Delhi: Farooq Abdullah
content: Commenting on U.S. announcing a ‘waiver’ to India for importing Russian oil, the National Conference leader states that India is a sovereign country and does not need anyone’s permission

title: Education is key to women’s empowerment, says A.P. Governor
content: Governor Syed Abdul Nazeer gives away degrees to 2,591 students at 22nd convocation at SPMVV in Tirupati; DRDO former scientist Sasikala Sinha conferred with honorary doctorate

title: CPI(M) stages protest against State Budget in Kalaburagi
content: ₹4.48 lakh crore State Budget, presented as a pro-people Budget aimed at the development of all sections, in reality favours private and corporate interests, says CPI(M) district secretary Neela

title: E-health system launched in Edamalakkudy health centre
content: patients from Edamalakkudy can now schedule online appointments at the Adimaly Taluk Hospital, the District Hospital, or the Medical College Hospital

title: CBI initiates probe into alleged tampering of BSF records to falsely implicate two constables in smuggling case
content: In its order dated September 8, 2025, the Delhi High Court has said there was ‘sufficient material to reach a prima facie conclusion’ that an in-depth probe was required into the matter

title: Samrat Choudhary, top prospect for Bihar’s next CM, to meet Amit Shah in West Bengal on March 8
content: Duo likely to address a public meeting together in Bagdogra; RJD’s Rabri Devi says Nitish Kumar should not leave Bihar; Nishant Kumar meets JD(U) legislators on the eve of formally joining the party

title: ACB officials seize assets of Giddaluru in-charge Sub-Registrar
content: Raids conducted at Chirala town and Nanduru village of Ponnuru mandal on the assets of the accused SRO and his relatives in Guntur district

title: Forensic probe concludes adulteration of milk with ethylene glycol, says East Godavari Collector Kirthi Chekuri
content: Ten persons died of acute renal failure so far and 10 others, including three infants, are battling for life after consuming the milk supplied by a vendor on February 15 and 16 in Rajamahendravaram

title: From traffic junctions to night patrols, Hyderabad’s women in uniform take the frontline
content: For these women in uniform, directing traffic, solving cases and showing up for duty, every day is already Women’s Day

title: Women’s collective warns parties to ensure 33% women candidates for Assembly polls or face NOTA
content: Thulya Prathinidhya Prasthanam raises five demands ahead of Assembly polls, calls for gender-balanced politics in Kerala.

title: ‘Able Points’ to offer jobs for differently abled
content: ‘Able Point’ will sell products made by the differently abled. The plan is to ensure a monthly salary of not less than ₹15,000 for the employees

title: Secularism can sweep away superstition like a storm, says K.E.N. Kunhahammed
content: Speakers at Thrissur Secular Forum meet call for stronger rationalist laws and defence of constitutional values

title: Saina Nehwal urges students to focus on fitness and discipline

title: Girl in frame and an aunt in shadows: Could North Korea get its first female supreme leader?
content: north Korea remains a sealed stage for most of the world . apart from routine missile launches, grand military parades and the carefully filtered images released by state media, the inner workings of Pyongyang’s ruling elite remain deeply opaque . rarely does a single photograph disrupt that familiar script .

title: Former Nepal PM KP Sharma Oli Extends Greetings To Balen Shah On Poll Win
content: Former Prime Minister of Nepal KP Sharma Oli extended his wishes to Balen Shah on securing victory in the latest parliamentary elections.

title: Explosion Hits US Embassy In Oslo, Causes Minor Damage, Thick Smoke
content: The blast was heard from the embassy compound in western Oslo at around 1 am (0000 GMT), and smoke was seen rising from the area, eyewitnesses said.

title: 'AI Key In National Security But...': OpenAI Robotic Head Quits Over Pentagon Deal
content: OpenAI struck its deal with the Pentagon in late February following the breakdown of talks between the Trump administration and Anthropic PBC.

title: "BJP Misusing Country's Highest Chair": Mamata Banerjee Amid President Row
content: West Bengal Chief Minister and Trinamool Congress leader Mamata Banerjee has accused the ruling BJP at the Centre of disrespecting and misusing the highest chair in the country for its agenda after President Droupadi Murmu expressed displeasure

title: 'No One Will Be Left To Say 'We Surrender'": Trump Attacks Iran President
content: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian had dismissed Donald Trump's demand for the Islamic Republic's unconditional surrender as "a dream".

title: Kuwait Airport Targeted, Saudi Intercepts Drones As Iran Continues Attacks
content: Fuel tanks at the Kuwait airport were hit while 15 drones were intercepted and destroyed in Saudi Arabia as Iran continued attacks a day after its President Masoud Pezkeshian said Tehran will not target neighbouring countries.

title: US-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Iran Says It Can Fight 'Intense War' For Six Months
content: The war in the Middle East was triggered after the US and Israel attacked Iran last Saturday, killing Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

title: 'Take Pressure Off': Trump On Easing Oil Prices After Russian Waiver To India
content: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Friday said the US was issuing a temporary 30-day waiver to allow Indian refiners to purchase Russian oil.

title: 'Will Remember Lack Of British Support During Iran War': Trump Slams Starmer
content: Donald Trump has repeatedly criticised Keir Starmer after the UK blocked the US's initial use of British bases to attack Iran.

title: "Let's Go For Final Drive": YouTuber Anurag Dobhal Livestreams Crash At 150kmph
content: Dobhal, popularly known as UK07 rider, is one of India's best-known motovloggers and was also a contestant on Bigg Boss.

title: Pak Driver Killed As Debris From Aerial Interception Falls On Vehicle In Dubai
content: Dubai Media Office has confirmed that debris from an aerial interception fell onto a vehicle in the Al Barsha area, resulting in the death of a Pakistani driver.

title: "They Have No Accuracy": Trump Blames Iran For Deadly School Strike
content: A strike hit a girls' elementary school in Minab last Saturday, killing more than 150 people, mostly students.

title: Nepal Election 2026 Results Highlights: Rapper-Politician Balen Shah's RSP Registers Landslide Victory
content: Rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah's Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) is all set to form the next government in Nepal after securing a sweeping victory in the crucial general election on Saturday.

title: Trump Started War Against Iran. He's Still Figuring Out How It Ends
content: A week into the Iran war, what Trump actually wants remains, at best, unclear, and his own statements suggest even he may not know.

title: PM Modi Calls Om Birla An "Excellent Speaker" Ahead Of No-Confidence Motion
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday described Om Birla as an outstanding Member of Parliament and "excellent" Speaker of the Lok Sabha, praising him for functioning above party lines and carrying everyone along while presiding over the House.

title: Trump's Warning Keeps Iran On Edge As Dubai, Doha Report Explosions Again
content: US President Donald Trump had threatened Iran on Saturday with expanding strikes to include new targets and said that America would hit Iran "very hard".

title: "Indian Ocean Must Remain Peace Zone": Lankan Opposition Leader To NDTV
content: Speaking with NDTV, Sajith Premadasa, Leader of Opposition in Sri Lanka emphasised the critical need for the Indian Ocean region to remain peaceful and not become part of any conflict.

title: Smoke Rises From Tower In Dubai Marina As Iran Continues Gulf Attacks
content: Debris from an intercepted Iranian projectile struck a tower in Dubai Marina on Saturday, leaving smoke rising from its facade.

title: Centre Dismisses "Fake" Social Media Claims Of New Union Territory Plan
content: The government on Saturday dismissed as false the claims circulating on social media that the Centre is planning to create a new Union Territory by combining certain districts from Bihar and West Bengal.

title: BJP Leader Suspended After Illegal Opium Cultivation Found In Chhattisgarh Farmhouse
content: A political storm has erupted in Chhattisgarh after a BJP leader from Durg district was caught allegedly cultivating opium on more than one and a half acres of land at his farmhouse.
